heading: 9.7.14.5.6.Matrix Fragments formma.m16n8k4
content:
A warp executing `mma.m16n8k4` will compute an MMA operation of shape `.m16n8k4`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A:

  + `.tf32`:

    | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
    | --- | --- | --- |
    | `.tf32` | A vector expression containing two `.b32` registers, containing two `.tf32` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1 |

    The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 65](#mma-1684-a-tf32).

    



[Figure: mma-1684-A]
MMA .m16n8k4 Fragment Layout for Matrix A with .f64 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Instruction: MMA .m16n8k4
   Matrix: A (input matrix)
   Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)
   Matrix A logical dimensions: 16 rows x 4 columns (M=16, K=4)

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   The table has rows labeled by matrix row index (0-15) and columns labeled 0-3 (Col 0 through Col 3).
   Columns 0-3 represent the 4 column positions (K=4 dimension) of matrix A.
   The notation is: Tx:ay where x = thread lane ID, y = register fragment index (a0 or a1).

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (complete, per row)

   UPPER HALF (matrix rows 0-7, register fragment a0):

   Row 0:  Col0=T0:a0,  Col1=T1:a0,  Col2=T2:a0,  Col3=T3:a0
   Row 1:  Col0=T4:a0,  Col1=T5:a0,  Col2=T6:a0,  Col3=T7:a0
   Row 2:  (pattern continues with arrows indicating same thread/register assignment pattern)
   Rows 2-6: implied continuation of the same pattern (T8-T27 covering rows 2-6 in groups of 4 threads per row)
   Row 7:  Col0=T28:a0, Col1=T29:a0, Col2=T30:a0, Col3=T31:a0

   LOWER HALF (matrix rows 8-15, register fragment a1):

   Row 8:  Col0=T0:a1,  Col1=T1:a1,  Col2=T2:a1,  Col3=T3:a1
   Row 9:  Col0=T4:a1,  Col1=T5:a1,  Col2=T6:a1,  Col3=T7:a1
   Rows 10-14: implied continuation of the same pattern (T8-T27 covering rows 10-14 in groups of 4 threads per row)
   Row 15: Col0=T28:a1, Col1=T29:a1, Col2=T30:a1, Col3=T31:a1

4. DATA TYPE
   .f64 — each element is a 64-bit double precision float.
   Each element occupies one 64-bit register (or a pair of 32-bit registers in PTX representation).

5. GROUPING PATTERN
   All 32 threads (T0-T31) participate.
   Each thread holds exactly 2 fragments: a0 and a1.
   Threads are grouped in sets of 4 consecutive threads per matrix row:
     T0-T3   -> matrix row 0  (fragment a0)
     T4-T7   -> matrix row 1  (fragment a0)
     T8-T11  -> matrix row 2  (fragment a0)
     T12-T15 -> matrix row 3  (fragment a0)
     T16-T19 -> matrix row 4  (fragment a0)
     T20-T23 -> matrix row 5  (fragment a0)
     T24-T27 -> matrix row 6  (fragment a0)
     T28-T31 -> matrix row 7  (fragment a0)
   The same thread grouping repeats for rows 8-15 using fragment a1:
     T0-T3   -> matrix row 8  (fragment a1)
     T4-T7   -> matrix row 9  (fragment a1)
     ...
     T28-T31 -> matrix row 15 (fragment a1)
   Within each group of 4 threads, each thread holds one of the 4 column elements (columns 0-3).

6. REGISTER PACKING
   Since the data type is .f64 (64-bit), each matrix element is stored in one dedicated 64-bit register fragment.
   There is NO packing of multiple values into a single register (unlike .f16 which packs two values per 32-bit register).
   Each thread holds 2 registers total for matrix A: a0 (one f64 element from the upper 8 rows) and a1 (one f64 element from the lower 8 rows).

   Index label: %laneid:{fragments} — the lane ID determines which column and row the thread's element corresponds to.

MMA .m16n8k4 Fragment Layout for Matrix A with .f64 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Instruction: MMA .m16n8k4
   Matrix: A (input matrix)
   Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)
   Matrix A logical dimensions: 16 rows x 4 columns (M=16, K=4)

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   The table has rows labeled by matrix row index (0-15) and columns labeled 0-3 (Col 0 through Col 3).
   Columns 0-3 represent the 4 column positions (K=4 dimension) of matrix A.
   The notation is: Tx:ay where x = thread lane ID, y = register fragment index (a0 or a1).

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (complete, per row)

   UPPER HALF (matrix rows 0-7, register fragment a0):

   Row 0:  Col0=T0:a0,  Col1=T1:a0,  Col2=T2:a0,  Col3=T3:a0
   Row 1:  Col0=T4:a0,  Col1=T5:a0,  Col2=T6:a0,  Col3=T7:a0
   Row 2:  (pattern continues with arrows indicating same thread/register assignment pattern)
   Rows 2-6: implied continuation of the same pattern (T8-T27 covering rows 2-6 in groups of 4 threads per row)
   Row 7:  Col0=T28:a0, Col1=T29:a0, Col2=T30:a0, Col3=T31:a0

   LOWER HALF (matrix rows 8-15, register fragment a1):

   Row 8:  Col0=T0:a1,  Col1=T1:a1,  Col2=T2:a1,  Col3=T3:a1
   Row 9:  Col0=T4:a1,  Col1=T5:a1,  Col2=T6:a1,  Col3=T7:a1
   Rows 10-14: implied continuation of the same pattern (T8-T27 covering rows 10-14 in groups of 4 threads per row)
   Row 15: Col0=T28:a1, Col1=T29:a1, Col2=T30:a1, Col3=T31:a1

4. DATA TYPE
   .f64 — each element is a 64-bit double precision float.
   Each element occupies one 64-bit register (or a pair of 32-bit registers in PTX representation).

5. GROUPING PATTERN
   All 32 threads (T0-T31) participate.
   Each thread holds exactly 2 fragments: a0 and a1.
   Threads are grouped in sets of 4 consecutive threads per matrix row:
     T0-T3   -> matrix row 0  (fragment a0)
     T4-T7   -> matrix row 1  (fragment a0)
     T8-T11  -> matrix row 2  (fragment a0)
     T12-T15 -> matrix row 3  (fragment a0)
     T16-T19 -> matrix row 4  (fragment a0)
     T20-T23 -> matrix row 5  (fragment a0)
     T24-T27 -> matrix row 6  (fragment a0)
     T28-T31 -> matrix row 7  (fragment a0)
   The same thread grouping repeats for rows 8-15 using fragment a1:
     T0-T3   -> matrix row 8  (fragment a1)
     T4-T7   -> matrix row 9  (fragment a1)
     ...
     T28-T31 -> matrix row 15 (fragment a1)
   Within each group of 4 threads, each thread holds one of the 4 column elements (columns 0-3).

6. REGISTER PACKING
   Since the data type is .f64 (64-bit), each matrix element is stored in one dedicated 64-bit register fragment.
   There is NO packing of multiple values into a single register (unlike .f16 which packs two values per 32-bit register).
   Each thread holds 2 registers total for matrix A: a0 (one f64 element from the upper 8 rows) and a1 (one f64 element from the lower 8 rows).

   Index label: %laneid:{fragments} — the lane ID determines which column and row the thread's element corresponds to.


    Figure 65 MMA .m16n8k4 fragment layout for matrix A with `.tf32` type.

    The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

    ```
    groupID           = %laneid >> 2
    threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

    row =      groupID            for a0
               groupID + 8        for a1

    col =  threadID_in_group
    ```
  + `.f64`:

    > | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
    > | --- | --- | --- |
    > | `.f64` | A vector expression containing two `.f64` registers, containing two `.f64` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1 |
    >
    > The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 66](#mma-1684-a-f64).
    >
    > 



[Figure: mma-1684-A]
MMA .m16n8k4 Fragment Layout for Matrix A with .f64 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Instruction: MMA .m16n8k4
   Matrix: A (input matrix)
   Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)
   Matrix A logical dimensions: 16 rows x 4 columns (M=16, K=4)

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   The table has rows labeled by matrix row index (0-15) and columns labeled 0-3 (Col 0 through Col 3).
   Columns 0-3 represent the 4 column positions (K=4 dimension) of matrix A.
   The notation is: Tx:ay where x = thread lane ID, y = register fragment index (a0 or a1).

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (complete, per row)

   UPPER HALF (matrix rows 0-7, register fragment a0):

   Row 0:  Col0=T0:a0,  Col1=T1:a0,  Col2=T2:a0,  Col3=T3:a0
   Row 1:  Col0=T4:a0,  Col1=T5:a0,  Col2=T6:a0,  Col3=T7:a0
   Row 2:  (pattern continues with arrows indicating same thread/register assignment pattern)
   Rows 2-6: implied continuation of the same pattern (T8-T27 covering rows 2-6 in groups of 4 threads per row)
   Row 7:  Col0=T28:a0, Col1=T29:a0, Col2=T30:a0, Col3=T31:a0

   LOWER HALF (matrix rows 8-15, register fragment a1):

   Row 8:  Col0=T0:a1,  Col1=T1:a1,  Col2=T2:a1,  Col3=T3:a1
   Row 9:  Col0=T4:a1,  Col1=T5:a1,  Col2=T6:a1,  Col3=T7:a1
   Rows 10-14: implied continuation of the same pattern (T8-T27 covering rows 10-14 in groups of 4 threads per row)
   Row 15: Col0=T28:a1, Col1=T29:a1, Col2=T30:a1, Col3=T31:a1

4. DATA TYPE
   .f64 — each element is a 64-bit double precision float.
   Each element occupies one 64-bit register (or a pair of 32-bit registers in PTX representation).

5. GROUPING PATTERN
   All 32 threads (T0-T31) participate.
   Each thread holds exactly 2 fragments: a0 and a1.
   Threads are grouped in sets of 4 consecutive threads per matrix row:
     T0-T3   -> matrix row 0  (fragment a0)
     T4-T7   -> matrix row 1  (fragment a0)
     T8-T11  -> matrix row 2  (fragment a0)
     T12-T15 -> matrix row 3  (fragment a0)
     T16-T19 -> matrix row 4  (fragment a0)
     T20-T23 -> matrix row 5  (fragment a0)
     T24-T27 -> matrix row 6  (fragment a0)
     T28-T31 -> matrix row 7  (fragment a0)
   The same thread grouping repeats for rows 8-15 using fragment a1:
     T0-T3   -> matrix row 8  (fragment a1)
     T4-T7   -> matrix row 9  (fragment a1)
     ...
     T28-T31 -> matrix row 15 (fragment a1)
   Within each group of 4 threads, each thread holds one of the 4 column elements (columns 0-3).

6. REGISTER PACKING
   Since the data type is .f64 (64-bit), each matrix element is stored in one dedicated 64-bit register fragment.
   There is NO packing of multiple values into a single register (unlike .f16 which packs two values per 32-bit register).
   Each thread holds 2 registers total for matrix A: a0 (one f64 element from the upper 8 rows) and a1 (one f64 element from the lower 8 rows).

   Index label: %laneid:{fragments} — the lane ID determines which column and row the thread's element corresponds to.

MMA .m16n8k4 Fragment Layout for Matrix A with .f64 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Instruction: MMA .m16n8k4
   Matrix: A (input matrix)
   Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)
   Matrix A logical dimensions: 16 rows x 4 columns (M=16, K=4)

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   The table has rows labeled by matrix row index (0-15) and columns labeled 0-3 (Col 0 through Col 3).
   Columns 0-3 represent the 4 column positions (K=4 dimension) of matrix A.
   The notation is: Tx:ay where x = thread lane ID, y = register fragment index (a0 or a1).

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (complete, per row)

   UPPER HALF (matrix rows 0-7, register fragment a0):

   Row 0:  Col0=T0:a0,  Col1=T1:a0,  Col2=T2:a0,  Col3=T3:a0
   Row 1:  Col0=T4:a0,  Col1=T5:a0,  Col2=T6:a0,  Col3=T7:a0
   Row 2:  (pattern continues with arrows indicating same thread/register assignment pattern)
   Rows 2-6: implied continuation of the same pattern (T8-T27 covering rows 2-6 in groups of 4 threads per row)
   Row 7:  Col0=T28:a0, Col1=T29:a0, Col2=T30:a0, Col3=T31:a0

   LOWER HALF (matrix rows 8-15, register fragment a1):

   Row 8:  Col0=T0:a1,  Col1=T1:a1,  Col2=T2:a1,  Col3=T3:a1
   Row 9:  Col0=T4:a1,  Col1=T5:a1,  Col2=T6:a1,  Col3=T7:a1
   Rows 10-14: implied continuation of the same pattern (T8-T27 covering rows 10-14 in groups of 4 threads per row)
   Row 15: Col0=T28:a1, Col1=T29:a1, Col2=T30:a1, Col3=T31:a1

4. DATA TYPE
   .f64 — each element is a 64-bit double precision float.
   Each element occupies one 64-bit register (or a pair of 32-bit registers in PTX representation).

5. GROUPING PATTERN
   All 32 threads (T0-T31) participate.
   Each thread holds exactly 2 fragments: a0 and a1.
   Threads are grouped in sets of 4 consecutive threads per matrix row:
     T0-T3   -> matrix row 0  (fragment a0)
     T4-T7   -> matrix row 1  (fragment a0)
     T8-T11  -> matrix row 2  (fragment a0)
     T12-T15 -> matrix row 3  (fragment a0)
     T16-T19 -> matrix row 4  (fragment a0)
     T20-T23 -> matrix row 5  (fragment a0)
     T24-T27 -> matrix row 6  (fragment a0)
     T28-T31 -> matrix row 7  (fragment a0)
   The same thread grouping repeats for rows 8-15 using fragment a1:
     T0-T3   -> matrix row 8  (fragment a1)
     T4-T7   -> matrix row 9  (fragment a1)
     ...
     T28-T31 -> matrix row 15 (fragment a1)
   Within each group of 4 threads, each thread holds one of the 4 column elements (columns 0-3).

6. REGISTER PACKING
   Since the data type is .f64 (64-bit), each matrix element is stored in one dedicated 64-bit register fragment.
   There is NO packing of multiple values into a single register (unlike .f16 which packs two values per 32-bit register).
   Each thread holds 2 registers total for matrix A: a0 (one f64 element from the upper 8 rows) and a1 (one f64 element from the lower 8 rows).

   Index label: %laneid:{fragments} — the lane ID determines which column and row the thread's element corresponds to.

    >
    >
    > Figure 66 MMA .m16n8k4 fragment layout for matrix A with `.f64` type.
    >
    > The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:
    >
    > ```
    > groupID           = %laneid >> 2
    > threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4
    >
    > row =      groupID            for a0
    >            groupID + 8        for a1
    >
    > col =  threadID_in_group
    > ```
* Multiplicand B:

  + `.tf32`:

    > | .btype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
    > | --- | --- | --- |
    > | `.tf32` | A vector expression of a single `.b32` register, containing a single `.tf32` element from the matrix B. | b0 |
    >
    > The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 67](#mma-1684-b-tf32).
    >
    > 



[Figure: mma-1684-B]
MMA .m16n8k4 Fragment Layout for Matrix B with .f64 Type

1. MATRIX IDENTIFICATION
   - Instruction: mma.m16n8k4
   - Matrix: B (input matrix)
   - Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)
   - Matrix B dimensions in this operation: K x N = 4 rows x 8 columns

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   - Rows represent matrix rows (0 through 3), total of 4 rows (K=4 dimension)
   - Columns represent matrix columns (0 through 7), total of 8 columns (N=8 dimension)
   - The table header shows: Row\Column | 0 | 1 | 2 | .. | 7
   - Columns 2 through 6 are implied by the ".." notation with an arrow indicating continuation

3. THREAD AND REGISTER ASSIGNMENT (complete mapping)

   Row 0, Column 0: Thread T0,  register b0
   Row 0, Column 1: Thread T4,  register b0
   Row 0, Column 2: (implied continuation)
   Row 0, Column 7: Thread T28, register b0

   Row 1, Column 0: Thread T1,  register b0
   Row 1, Column 1: Thread T5,  register b0
   Row 1, Column 2: (implied continuation)
   Row 1, Column 7: Thread T29, register b0

   Row 2, Column 0: Thread T2,  register b0
   Row 2, Column 1: Thread T6,  register b0
   Row 2, Column 2: (implied continuation)
   Row 2, Column 7: Thread T30, register b0

   Row 3, Column 0: Thread T3,  register b0
   Row 3, Column 1: Thread T7,  register b0
   Row 3, Column 2: (implied continuation)
   Row 3, Column 7: Thread T31, register b0

4. GROUPING PATTERN
   - Threads T0-T3 cover column 0, rows 0-3 respectively, each holding b0
   - Threads T4-T7 cover column 1, rows 0-3 respectively, each holding b0
   - Pattern continues: threads T(4*c) through T(4*c+3) cover column c, rows 0-3
   - Threads T28-T31 cover column 7, rows 0-3 respectively, each holding b0
   - Each group of 4 consecutive threads (T0-T3, T4-T7, ..., T28-T31) maps to one column of matrix B
   - Total threads used: T0 through T31 (32 threads, one full warp)

5. REGISTER USAGE PER THREAD
   - Each thread holds exactly one register fragment: b0
   - Since the data type is .f64 (64-bit), each b0 register holds one 64-bit double-precision value
   - Each thread stores exactly one matrix element of matrix B

6. REGISTER PACKING
   - Data type is .f64 (64-bit double precision)
   - One element per register (no packing of multiple values into a single register, unlike f16 which packs two values)
   - Each thread holds 1 register (b0) containing 1 f64 element

7. NOTATION
   - Format in figure: T{laneid}:b{register_index}
   - %laneid:{fragments} label at bottom confirms lane ID determines fragment assignment
   - The arrow in columns 2-6 area indicates the pattern continues uniformly across all intermediate columns

MMA .m16n8k4 Fragment Layout for Matrix B with .f64 Type

1. MATRIX IDENTIFICATION
   - Instruction: mma.m16n8k4
   - Matrix: B (input matrix)
   - Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)
   - Matrix B dimensions in this operation: K x N = 4 rows x 8 columns

2. GRID STRUCTURE

   - Columns represent matrix columns (0 through 7), total of 8 columns (N=8 dimension)
   - The table header shows: Row\Column | 0 | 1 | 2 | .. | 7
   - Columns 2 through 6 are implied by the ".." notation with an arrow indicating continuation

3. THREAD AND REGISTER ASSIGNMENT (complete mapping)

   Row 0, Column 0: Thread T0,  register b0
   Row 0, Column 1: Thread T4,  register b0
   Row 0, Column 2: (implied continuation)
   Row 0, Column 7: Thread T28, register b0

   Row 1, Column 0: Thread T1,  register b0

   Row 2, Column 0: Thread T2,  register b0
   Row 2, Column 1: Thread T6,  register b0
   Row 2, Column 2: (implied continuation)
   Row 2, Column 7: Thread T30, register b0

   Row 3, Column 0: Thread T3,  register b0
   Row 3, Column 1: Thread T7,  register b0
   Row 3, Column 2: (implied continuation)
   Row 3, Column 7: Thread T31, register b0

4. GROUPING PATTERN
   - Threads T0-T3 cover column 0, rows 0-3 respectively, each holding b0
   - Threads T4-T7 cover column 1, rows 0-3 respectively, each holding b0
   - Pattern continues: threads T(4*c) through T(4*c+3) cover column c, rows 0-3
   - Threads T28-T31 cover column 7, rows 0-3 respectively, each holding b0
   - Each group of 4 consecutive threads (T0-T3, T4-T7, ..., T28-T31) maps to one column of matrix B
   - Total threads used: T0 through T31 (32 threads, one full warp)

5. REGISTER USAGE PER THREAD
   - Each thread holds exactly one register fragment: b0
   - Since the data type is .f64 (64-bit), each b0 register holds one 64-bit double-precision value
   - Each thread stores exactly one matrix element of matrix B

6. REGISTER PACKING
   - Data type is .f64 (64-bit double precision)
   - One element per register (no packing of multiple values into a single register, unlike f16 which packs two values)
   - Each thread holds 1 register (b0) containing 1 f64 element

7. NOTATION
   - Format in figure: T{laneid}:b{register_index}
   - %laneid:{fragments} label at bottom confirms lane ID determines fragment assignment
   - The arrow in columns 2-6 area indicates the pattern continues uniformly across all intermediate columns

    >
    >
    > Figure 67 MMA .m16n8k4 fragment layout for matrix B with `.tf32` type.
    >
    > The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:
    >
    > ```
    > groupID           = %laneid >> 2
    > threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4
    >
    > row =  threadID_in_group
    >
    > col =  groupID
    > ```
  + `.f64`:

    > | .btype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
    > | --- | --- | --- |
    > | `.f64` | A vector expression of a single `.f64` register, containing a single `.f64` element from the matrix B. | b0 |
    >
    > The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 68](#mma-1684-b-f64).
    >
    > 



[Figure: mma-1684-B]
MMA .m16n8k4 Fragment Layout for Matrix B with .f64 Type

1. MATRIX IDENTIFICATION
   - Instruction: mma.m16n8k4
   - Matrix: B (input matrix)
   - Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)
   - Matrix B dimensions in this operation: K x N = 4 rows x 8 columns

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   - Rows represent matrix rows (0 through 3), total of 4 rows (K=4 dimension)
   - Columns represent matrix columns (0 through 7), total of 8 columns (N=8 dimension)
   - The table header shows: Row\Column | 0 | 1 | 2 | .. | 7
   - Columns 2 through 6 are implied by the ".." notation with an arrow indicating continuation

3. THREAD AND REGISTER ASSIGNMENT (complete mapping)

   Row 0, Column 0: Thread T0,  register b0

   Row 3, Column 7: Thread T31, register b0

4. GROUPING PATTERN
   - Threads T0-T3 cover column 0, rows 0-3 respectively, each holding b0
   - Threads T4-T7 cover column 1, rows 0-3 respectively, each holding b0
   - Pattern continues: threads T(4*c) through T(4*c+3) cover column c, rows 0-3
   - Threads T28-T31 cover column 7, rows 0-3 respectively, each holding b0
   - Each group of 4 consecutive threads (T0-T3, T4-T7, ..., T28-T31) maps to one column of matrix B
   - Total threads used: T0 through T31 (32 threads, one full warp)

5. REGISTER USAGE PER THREAD
   - Each thread holds exactly one register fragment: b0
   - Since the data type is .f64 (64-bit), each b0 register holds one 64-bit double-precision value
   - Each thread stores exactly one matrix element of matrix B

6. REGISTER PACKING
   - Data type is .f64 (64-bit double precision)
   - One element per register (no packing of multiple values into a single register, unlike f16 which packs two values)
   - Each thread holds 1 register (b0) containing 1 f64 element

7. NOTATION
   - Format in figure: T{laneid}:b{register_index}
   - %laneid:{fragments} label at bottom confirms lane ID determines fragment assignment
   - The arrow in columns 2-6 area indicates the pattern continues uniformly across all intermediate columns

MMA .m16n8k4 Fragment Layout for Matrix B with .f64 Type

1. MATRIX IDENTIFICATION
   - Instruction: mma.m16n8k4
   - Matrix: B (input matrix)
   - Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)
   - Matrix B dimensions in this operation: K x N = 4 rows x 8 columns

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   - Rows represent matrix rows (0 through 3), total of 4 rows (K=4 dimension)
   - Columns represent matrix columns (0 through 7), total of 8 columns (N=8 dimension)
   - The table header shows: Row\Column | 0 | 1 | 2 | .. | 7
   - Columns 2 through 6 are implied by the ".." notation with an arrow indicating continuation

3. THREAD AND REGISTER ASSIGNMENT (complete mapping)

   Row 0, Column 0: Thread T0,  register b0
   Row 0, Column 1: Thread T4,  register b0
   Row 0, Column 2: (implied continuation)
   Row 0, Column 7: Thread T28, register b0

   Row 1, Column 0: Thread T1,  register b0
   Row 1, Column 1: Thread T5,  register b0
   Row 1, Column 2: (implied continuation)
   Row 1, Column 7: Thread T29, register b0

   Row 2, Column 0: Thread T2,  register b0
   Row 2, Column 1: Thread T6,  register b0
   Row 2, Column 2: (implied continuation)
   Row 2, Column 7: Thread T30, register b0

   Row 3, Column 0: Thread T3,  register b0
   Row 3, Column 1: Thread T7,  register b0
   Row 3, Column 2: (implied continuation)
   Row 3, Column 7: Thread T31, register b0

4. GROUPING PATTERN
   - Threads T0-T3 cover column 0, rows 0-3 respectively, each holding b0
   - Threads T4-T7 cover column 1, rows 0-3 respectively, each holding b0
   - Pattern continues: threads T(4*c) through T(4*c+3) cover column c, rows 0-3
   - Threads T28-T31 cover column 7, rows 0-3 respectively, each holding b0
   - Each group of 4 consecutive threads (T0-T3, T4-T7, ..., T28-T31) maps to one column of matrix B
   - Total threads used: T0 through T31 (32 threads, one full warp)

5. REGISTER USAGE PER THREAD
   - Each thread holds exactly one register fragment: b0
   - Since the data type is .f64 (64-bit), each b0 register holds one 64-bit double-precision value
   - Each thread stores exactly one matrix element of matrix B

6. REGISTER PACKING
   - Data type is .f64 (64-bit double precision)
   - One element per register (no packing of multiple values into a single register, unlike f16 which packs two values)
   - Each thread holds 1 register (b0) containing 1 f64 element

7. NOTATION
   - Format in figure: T{laneid}:b{register_index}
   - %laneid:{fragments} label at bottom confirms lane ID determines fragment assignment
   - The arrow in columns 2-6 area indicates the pattern continues uniformly across all intermediate columns

    >
    >
    > Figure 68 MMA .m16n8k4 fragment layout for matrix B with `.f64` type.
    >
    > The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:
    >
    > ```
    > groupID           = %laneid >> 2
    > threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4
    >
    > row =  threadID_in_group
    >
    > col =  groupID
    > ```
* Accumulators (C or D):

  + `.tf32`:

    > | .ctype / .dtype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
    > | --- | --- | --- |
    > | `.f32` | A vector expression containing four `.f32` registers, containing four `.f32` elements from the matrix C (or D). | c0, c1, c2, c3 |
    >
    > The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 69](#mma-1684-c-f32).
    >
    > 



[Figure: mma-1684-C]
MMA .m16n8k4 Fragment Layout for Accumulator Matrix C/D with .f64 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND TYPE
   Instruction: mma.m16n8k4
   Matrix: C/D (accumulator)
   Dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=4
   Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   The table has rows labeled 0-15 (matrix rows, with intermediate rows implied by arrows and ".." notation) and columns labeled 0-7 (matrix columns).
   Columns are paired: {0,1}, {2,3}, {4,5}, {6,7} — each pair is covered by one thread entry.
   Threads T0 through T31 (32 threads total) are used.

3. REGISTER ELEMENT ASSIGNMENT PER THREAD

   UPPER HALF OF MATRIX (rows 0-7), registers c0 and c1:

   Row 0,  Cols 0-1:  T0:  {c0, c1}
   Row 0,  Cols 2-3:  T1:  {c0, c1}
   Row 0,  Cols 4-5:  T2:  {c0, c1}
   Row 0,  Cols 6-7:  T3:  {c0, c1}

   Row 1,  Cols 0-1:  T4:  {c0, c1}
   Row 1,  Cols 2-3:  T5:  {c0, c1}
   Row 1,  Cols 4-5:  T6:  {c0, c1}
   Row 1,  Cols 6-7:  T7:  {c0, c1}

   Rows 2-6 follow the same pattern (implied by arrows and ".." notation), continuing with T8-T27 in groups of 4 threads per row, each thread holding {c0, c1}.

   Row 7,  Cols 0-1:  T28: {c0, c1}
   Row 7,  Cols 2-3:  T29: {c0, c1}
   Row 7,  Cols 4-5:  T30: {c0, c1}
   Row 7,  Cols 6-7:  T31: {c0, c1}

   LOWER HALF OF MATRIX (rows 8-15), registers c2 and c3:

   Row 8,  Cols 0-1:  T0:  {c2, c3}
   Row 8,  Cols 2-3:  T1:  {c2, c3}
   Row 8,  Cols 4-5:  T2:  {c2, c3}
   Row 8,  Cols 6-7:  T3:  {c2, c3}

   Row 9,  Cols 0-1:  T4:  {c2, c3}
   Row 9,  Cols 2-3:  T5:  {c2, c3}
   Row 9,  Cols 4-5:  T6:  {c2, c3}
   Row 9,  Cols 6-7:  T7:  {c2, c3}

   Rows 10-14 follow the same pattern (implied by arrows and ".." notation), continuing with T8-T27 in groups of 4 threads per row, each thread holding {c2, c3}.

   Row 15, Cols 0-1:  T28: {c2, c3}
   Row 15, Cols 2-3:  T29: {c2, c3}
   Row 15, Cols 4-5:  T30: {c2, c3}
   Row 15, Cols 6-7:  T31: {c2, c3}

4. GROUPING PATTERNS
   Each thread covers exactly 2 columns of the matrix at a given row.
   Threads T0-T3 handle row 0 (cols 0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7 respectively).
   Threads T4-T7 handle row 1.
   Each subsequent group of 4 threads (T8-T11, T12-T15, T16-T19, T20-T23, T24-T27) handles rows 2-6 respectively.
   Threads T28-T31 handle row 7.
   The same thread assignments repeat for rows 8-15, but using registers c2 and c3 instead of c0 and c1.
   Each thread T0-T31 therefore owns 4 registers total: c0, c1 (for the upper 8 rows) and c2, c3 (for the lower 8 rows).

5. PACKING WITHIN REGISTERS
   Each element is .f64 (64-bit double precision).
   Each register c0, c1, c2, c3 holds one f64 value (one matrix element).
   No packing of multiple values per register — each f64 element occupies its own register (or register pair in 32-bit register file terms, i.e., each f64 uses two 32-bit registers).
   The notation {c0, c1} means two separate f64 registers, each holding one matrix element.

6. LABEL AT BOTTOM
   %laneid:{fragments} — indicating the lane ID maps to the fragment registers as described above.

    >

    >
    > ```
    > groupID           = %laneid >> 2
    > threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

    > row =      groupID                            for c0 and c1
    >          groupID + 8                          for c2 and c3
    >
    > col =  (threadID_in_group * 2) + (i & 0x1)    for ci   where i = {0,..,3}
    > ```
